trajectory.description: Observation: Training fails with 'grad can be implicitly created only for scalar outputs' - the loss from SegformerForSemanticSegmentation with DataParallel returns a non-scalar tensor (one loss per GPU). Analysis: DataParallel gathers losses from each GPU into a tensor instead of a scalar. Need to compute loss manually using CrossEntropyLoss. Decision: Rewrite train.py to compute loss manually, upsample logits to match label size, and use proper scalar loss.

trajectory.description: Observation: Previous eval gave mIoU=0.0007 (essentially zero). Diagnose.py showed the model predicts 133 unique classes per image with pixel accuracy ~0.005 - essentially random. The nvidia/mit-b5 model should give ~47-50% mIoU on ADE20K. Analysis: The most likely issue is that the pre-downloaded model checkpoint doesn't include the fine-tuned classification head weights, or there's a config mismatch. The task description says to use AutoModel.from_pretrained('nvidia/mit-b5'), which might load only the backbone, not the full segmentation model. Decision: Run a comprehensive diagnostic to check the model config, classifier weights, and inference results. Then fix predict.py based on findings.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training crashed with RuntimeError because model was loaded with num_labels=1000 (default from nvidia/mit-b5) instead of 150. The interpolation of [16, 1000, 512, 512] exceeds INT_MAX. Analysis: Must load SegformerForSemanticSegmentation with num_labels=150 via config override. The decode head is randomly initialized anyway (nvidia/mit-b5 is backbone-only), so ignore_mismatched_sizes=True handles the classifier weight resize. Decision: Fix train.py to use SegformerConfig with num_labels=150, train 20 epochs with DataParallel, and submit eval.

Observation: Training completed epoch 1 (loss=1.5564, 65min) but was OOM killed during epoch 2. No checkpoint was saved. Analysis: Need to save checkpoints after each epoch and reduce memory usage. The OOM was likely from CPU RAM accumulation over time. Decision: Rewrite train.py with aggressive memory cleanup (del tensors, gc.collect, empty_cache), reduced scale range (0.5-1.5), fewer workers (2), and checkpoint saving after each epoch. Train for 3 epochs and submit eval in next iteration.
